text: The surgeon places trocars in the abdominal wall, establishing access points for the laparoscopic robotic instruments.

The surgeon begins the procedure by mobilizing the short gastric vessels, starting from the mid portion of the splenic hilum and extending to the gastroesophageal junction, using laparoscopic robotic assistance.
The surgeon uses a bipolar device and hook to perform surgical maneuvers at the gastroesophageal junction.
The surgeon uses a hook to dissect the left pillar of the crura, revealing the anatomical structure and separating it from surrounding tissues.
The surgeon resects the fat pad to achieve complete exposure of the gastric fundus, revealing the anatomical structure for further surgical manipulation.
The surgeon exposes the posterior aspect of the stomach, identifying a lesion that appears mostly intraluminal.
An intraoperative endoscopy reveals a lesion located approximately three centimeters from the gastroesophageal junction.
The surgeon inserts a 52 French bougie into the esophagus, which is visible as a flexible tube passing through the surgical field.
The surgeon performs an anterior gastrotomy approximately four centimeters from the gastroesophageal junction, creating an opening in the stomach wall.
The surgeon lifts the tumor from the posterior aspect of the gastric wall, revealing its position relative to the surrounding tissue.
Two endogea purple load staplers are fired across the gastric tissue, achieving a good margin of mucosa around the lesion.
The surgeon extracts the lesion using an endopouch and prepares it for histopathological analysis.
The frozen section analysis reveals a negative margin, showing no infiltration at the suture line.
The surgeon over-sews the suture line using three PDS sutures, reinforcing the closure with precise stitching.
The surgeon closes the anterior gastrotomy using running 3-0 VLOG sutures, followed by interrupted 3-0 PDS sutures.
An intraoperative endoscopy reveals no intragastric bleeding or leaks, and a clear passage through the gastroesophageal junction.
